content_en:
The entries on the major Companions — Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, Ali, Aisha, Abu Hurayrah, Ibn Masud, Ibn Abbas, and others — are comprehensive biographical treatments that draw together information scattered across the entire hadith and historical literature. For these central figures, Ibn Hajar provides detailed accounts of their lives, their relationships with the Prophet, their most important narrations and scholarly opinions, and the scholarly assessments of their character and reliability. These entries remain the standard references for biographical information about the major Companions.

The entries on less prominent Companions are equally valuable precisely because the information they contain is otherwise scattered across many sources. For the hundreds of Companions who played supporting roles in the history of early Islam — those who participated in the Battles of Badr or Uhud, those who made the emigration to Abyssinia, those who received the Prophet's blessing or heard a specific hadith — the Al-Isabah often provides the only comprehensive treatment available in a single place.

The sections on female Companions (Sahabiyyat) are particularly important. Women played significant roles in early Islam as transmitters of prophetic teaching, participants in significant events, and social actors in the early Muslim community, but the biographical literature has historically paid less attention to them. Ibn Hajar's treatment of female Companions is comprehensive and draws on specialized sources for women's biographies, preserving information that might otherwise be accessible only to specialists.

A persistent theme is the theological and legal importance of the Companions' status. Within the Islamic scholarly tradition, the reliability of the entire corpus of hadith literature depends on the Companions' trustworthiness as transmitters. Ibn Hajar's careful documentation of who the Companions were and the evidence for their status is not merely biographical curiosity but a foundation for the entire edifice of Islamic religious law and practice.

The entries also document the geographical spread of the early Muslim community. Companions settled throughout the expanding Islamic world — in Medina, Mecca, Syria, Iraq, Egypt, Yemen, and Persia — and Ibn Hajar's accounts note where each person settled, contributing to understanding of the early spread of Islam and the social geography of the early Muslim community.

The treatment of apparent contradictions in the sources — when different reports give different names, dates, or details for the same individual — is one of the most technically demanding aspects of the work. Ibn Hajar's resolutions of these contradictions demonstrate his mastery of the hadith sciences and historical methodology.
content_id:
Entri-entri tentang Sahabat-Sahabat utama — Abu Bakar, Umar, Utsman, Ali, Aisyah, Abu Hurayrah, Ibnu Mas'ud, Ibnu Abbas, dan lainnya — adalah perlakuan biografis yang komprehensif yang mengumpulkan informasi yang tersebar di seluruh literatur hadis dan historis. Untuk tokoh-tokoh sentral ini, Ibnu Hajar menyediakan catatan terperinci tentang kehidupan mereka, hubungan mereka dengan Nabi ﷺ, riwayat-riwayat terpenting dan pendapat-pendapat ilmiah mereka, serta penilaian ilmiah tentang karakter dan keandalan mereka.

Entri-entri tentang Sahabat yang kurang terkemuka sama-sama berharga justru karena informasi yang dikandungnya tersebar di banyak sumber. Untuk ratusan Sahabat yang memainkan peran pendukung dalam sejarah Islam awal, Al-Isabah seringkali menyediakan satu-satunya perlakuan komprehensif yang tersedia di satu tempat.

Bagian-bagian tentang Sahabat wanita (Sahabiyyat) sangat penting. Wanita memainkan peran signifikan dalam Islam awal sebagai perawi pengajaran kenabian, peserta dalam peristiwa-peristiwa penting, dan aktor sosial dalam komunitas Muslim awal, tetapi literatur biografi secara historis memberikan perhatian yang lebih sedikit kepada mereka. Perlakuan Ibnu Hajar tentang Sahabat wanita bersifat komprehensif dan menggunakan sumber-sumber khusus untuk biografi wanita.

Tema yang terus-menerus muncul adalah pentingnya status Sahabat secara teologis dan hukum. Dalam tradisi keilmuan Islam, keandalan seluruh korpus literatur hadis bergantung pada kepercayaan para Sahabat sebagai perawi. Dokumentasi Ibnu Hajar yang cermat tentang siapa para Sahabat dan bukti untuk status mereka bukan sekadar keingintahuan biografis tetapi fondasi bagi seluruh bangunan hukum dan praktik agama Islam.

Entri-entri juga mendokumentasikan sebaran geografis komunitas Muslim awal. Para Sahabat menetap di seluruh dunia Islam yang berkembang — di Madinah, Mekkah, Suriah, Irak, Mesir, Yaman, dan Persia — dan catatan Ibnu Hajar mencatat di mana masing-masing orang menetap, berkontribusi pada pemahaman tentang penyebaran awal Islam.
content_ar: The entries on the major الصحابة — أبو بكر, عمر, عثمان, علي, Aisha, أبو هريرة, Ibn Masud, ابن عباس, and others — are comprehensive biographical treatments that draw together information scattered across the entire الحديث and historical literature. For these المحوري figures, Ibn Hajar provides detailed accounts of their lives, their relationships with النبي ﷺ, their most المهم narrations and العلمي opinions, and the العلمي assessments of their الأخلاق and reliability. These entries remain the standard references for biographical information about the major الصحابة. The entries on less prominent الصحابة are equally valuable precisely لأن the information they contain is otherwise scattered across many sources. For the hundreds of الصحابة who played supporting roles in the history of early الإسلام — those who participated in the Battles of Badr or Uhud, those who made the emigration to Abyssinia, those who received النبي ﷺ's البركة or heard a specific الحديث — the Al-Isabah often provides the only comprehensive treatment available in a single place. The sections on female الصحابة (Sahabiyyat) are particularly المهم. Women played المهم roles in early الإسلام as transmitters of prophetic teaching, participants in المهم events, and social actors in the early مسلم الجماعة, but the biographical literature has historically paid less attention to them. Ibn Hajar's treatment of female الصحابة is comprehensive and draws on specialized sources for women's biographies, preserving information that might otherwise be accessible only to specialists. A persistent theme is the theological and legal importance of the الصحابة' status. Within the Islamic العلمي التراث, the reliability of the entire corpus of الحديث literature depends on the الصحابة' trustworthiness as transmitters. Ibn Hajar's careful documentation of who the الصحابة were and the evidence for their status is not merely biographical curiosity but a foundation for the entire edifice of Islamic religious law and practice. The entries أيضاً document the geographical spread of the early مسلم الجماعة. الصحابة settled throughout the expanding Islamic الدنيا — in المدينة المنورة, مكة المكرمة, Syria, Iraq, Egypt, Yemen, and Persia — and Ibn Hajar's accounts note where each person settled, contributing to الفهم of the early spread of الإسلام and the social geography of the early مسلم الجماعة. The treatment of apparent contradictions in the sources — عندما different reports give different names, dates, or details for the same individual — is one of the most technically demanding aspects of the work. Ibn Hajar's resolutions of these contradictions demonstrate his mastery of the الحديث sciences and historical methodology.
